New Zealand, um, does deliver outcomes that are safe,
secure, clean and sustainable for all New
Zealanders. Um, the past year has been one of, um,
progress through collaboration and partnership with the sector, working
smarter and delivering results across a range of areas.
Um, the maritime sector, I would make the point, is key to New Zealand's
livelihood. 99% of our imports and exports
by volume and 79% by value go across our ports.
Uh, in particular, in '24-'25, we advanced
key priorities aligned with the government and ministerial expectations
and delivered on regulatory frameworks as part of our strategy.
This progress was made despite a levy shortfall, uh, which we
can talk about later, through careful planning and use of reserves and targeted
savings enabled by improvements to our operating
model. Uh, digitization has been and continues
to be a major focus. Uh, we've made good progress on making it
easier for those we regulate to interact with us
digitally. Our regulated parties can now report incidents
from any digital device through an easy-to-use digital
form. This, combined with improvements to how we triage and respond to
notifications, including after hours, has meant people receive a
timely and appropriate response.
We've al- also made good progress on digitizing seafarer
applications alongside changes to improved processes,
rules and policies, uh, always with input from the sector.
This work will be launched along with a simplified website early in
2026. Um, these initiatives reduce time and cost,
not just for regulated parties, but provide internal
efficiencies for Maritime New Zealand while delivering safety
outcomes. Uh, and in fact, we've recently even returned some of those
saf- uh, those savings to applicants, which, um, the
chief executive can talk about. We continued progress on our
journey to implement our new regulatory approach focused on preventing
harm. This has involved targeting our regulatory activity to the
areas of greatest harm to people, the marine environment, property and
economy. It's also meant deploying a broader range of corrective and
enforcement tools, creating better transparency about how
and why we carry out our role and importantly continuing to
strengthen our partnerships with the sector through multi-year
harm prevention programs of interventions.
Um, these harm prevention programs are delivering
results. Uh, fertili- f- fatalities in the commercial
sector have continued to decline since '21-'22.
Uh, we extended our health and safety designation across all 13
commercial ports, taking up an increased, um, responsibility
there and with the Port Health and Safety Leadership Group, we launched
the approved code of practice for loading and unloading of cargo at
ports and on ships to create ba- baseline safety
standards in ports. And I would say that, as a board, we're very proud
of the approach that our exec take in terms of, um,
a collaborative leadership approach with the sector in terms of getting
these sub-types of things into place very quickly and,
and with a, a, a s- an aligned sector.

risks. Our first winter campaign targeting recreational

season.

pathways for new and low-carbon technologies, including
lithium battery safety. We continue to,
uh, engage internationally to influence changes to the international
